name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk consistently promotes conspiracy theories, misinformation, and divisive rhetoric. He has a pattern of spreading falsehoods about various topics, including COVID-19, vaccines, climate change, and election results. Kirk frequently targets marginalized groups, including LGBTQ individuals, Muslims, and immigrants, making derogatory comments and promoting discriminatory policies. He has also shown a willingness to incite violence and undermine democratic institutions. Kirk's actions and statements demonstrate a disregard for fact-based information and a commitment to promoting a far-right ideology.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk promoted the Cultural Marxism conspiracy theory and called universities 'islands of totalitarianism'.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed on social media that a U.S. doctor said George Floyd's death should be ruled an overdose.
Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet containing false information about human trafficking arrests after a fact-checker pointed out the error originated from the conspiracy site 8chan
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'
Charlie Kirk spread falsehoods about voter fraud ahead of the 2020 U.S. presidential election
Charlie Kirk promoted false and disproven claims of fraud in the 2020 election
Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix, promoting false and disproven claims of fraud in the election
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 "buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for this president"
Charlie Kirk's organizations sent seven buses with 350 students to the January 6 event in Washington, D.C., which led to the U.S. Capitol attack.
Charlie Kirk said he was "getting 500 emails a minute calling for a civil war" in the lead-up to the storming of the U.S. Capitol.
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked and tried to murder her husband with a hammer
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination when appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A Brookings Institution study found Charlie Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.

ProPublica investigated TPUSA's finances and found that the organization made 'misleading financial claims', that the audits were not done by an independent auditor, and that the leaders had enriched themselves.
Charlie Kirk alleged that the WHO covered up information about the COVID-19 pandemic
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that hydroxychloroquine had proved to be '100% effective in treating the virus'
Charlie Kirk falsely stated that during the 2009 swine flu pandemic it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency
Charlie Kirk described the public health measure of social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity
Charlie Kirk made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients
Charlie Kirk refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'

Charlie Kirk told a congregation, 'The Bible says very clearly to 'Occupy until I come ' ', a verse often cited by followers of the Seven Mountain Mandate to assert that before Jesus returns evangelical Christians must dominate seven areas of society
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines
Charlie Kirk called mandatory requirements for students to take the COVID-19 vaccine 'medical apartheid'
Charlie Kirk told young women to go to college for the purpose of finding husbands and 'embrace their roles as mothers and homemakers'.

Charlie Kirk said on his podcast that Democrats wanted Americans to live where 'there is no cultural identity, where you live in sexual anarchy, where private property is a thing of the past, and the ruling class controls everything'.
Charlie Kirk routinely made anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay was an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction
Charlie Kirk called the separation of church and state in the United States a 'fabrication'

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'.

Charlie Kirk told young women at the TPUSA Young Women's Leadership Summit 2022 Conference that the 'biblical model' for women to pursue in romantic relationships is a partner who is 'a protector and a leader, and deep down, a vast majority of you agree'.
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'.

Charlie Kirk said 'One issue I think that is so against our senses, so against the natural law and, dare I say, a throbbing middle finger to God, is the transgender thing happening in America right now'.

Charlie Kirk said, 'I think it's worth it, I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights'

Charlie Kirk said that 'all men are created equal in the eyes of God, all men and women, but not all cultures are created equal' and that 'Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced' as an outgrowth of the Bible

Charlie Kirk said, 'One of the reasons we're living through a constitutional crisis is that we no longer have a Christian nation, but we have a Christian form of government , and they're incompatible. You cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population'
Charlie Kirk said at a Trump campaign rally, 'This is a Christian state. I'd like to see it stay that way'
Charlie Kirk called for Trump to propose a nationwide ban of gender-affirming care for transgender people.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment of doctors who perform gender-affirming care and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for them.

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for a post that celebrated Pride Month, arguing that she was being selective and quoted a Bible verse that says 'thou shall lay with another man shall be stoned to death'.
Charlie Kirk claimed there had been 'too many' transgender mass shooters in the United States over the last 10 years, despite statistical evidence that such shootings by transgender people are rare.
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, and opposed exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse
Charlie Kirk discussed the burning of pride flags and said that convictions for those arrested for doing donuts over Pride flags painted on public streets should be overturned.

Charlie Kirk and TPUSA's communication director Candace Owens presented a symphony of racist dog-whistles at the 2018 Western Conservative Summit, with Kirk asserting that the increasing presence of African Americans in American politics resulted in the Black community being 'worse off'
Charlie Kirk cited single motherhood in Chicago's Black community as a cause of gun violence, blaming the absence of a father from some Black households on 'a broken culture problem'
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC

Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips, and promoted false claims of disproportionate criminality among migrants.
Charlie Kirk called Mehdi Hasan to be deported and deplatformed over his views on the COVID-19 pandemic, calling him a 'neurotic lunatic'.
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped at a Missouri State University event

Charlie Kirk stated that affirmative-action picks, such as Joy Reid and Michelle Obama, 'do not have the brain processing power to otherwise be taken really seriously' and 'had to go steal a white person's slot to go be taken somewhat seriously'
Charlie Kirk said 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies'
Charlie Kirk suggested that Jewish donors control 'not just the colleges; it's the nonprofits, it's the movies, it's Hollywood, it's all of it'
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and as someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'
Charlie Kirk condemned the Civil Rights Act of 1964, calling its passage a 'huge mistake' and alleging that it had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'
Charlie Kirk said 'If I see a Black pilot, I'm going to be like, 'Boy, I hope he's qualified'
Charlie Kirk blamed DEI programs for national aviation issues
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' who was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race
Charlie Kirk said, 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'
Charlie Kirk said, 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white'

Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality' alongside a Fox News headline that falsely claimed: '7.2M illegals entered the U.S. under Biden administration, an amount greater than population of 36 states'
Charlie Kirk called Martin Luther King Jr. a 'mythological anti-racist creation of the 1960s' and a 'very flawed' man
Charlie Kirk stated that 'America does not need more visas for people from India'
Charlie Kirk commented that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians
Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism
Charlie Kirk posted that '24 years ago a group of Muslims killed 2,753 people on 9/11. Now a Muslim Socialist is on pace to run New York City'
Charlie Kirk argued that 'It's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize our civilization'
Charlie Kirk texted associates that 'Jewish donors play into all the stereotypes'
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk from charges of antisemitism by claiming the post's charge against Jewish communities was accurate
Charlie Kirk claimed 'the philosophical foundation of anti-whiteness has been largely financed by Jewish donors'
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the Central Texas floods on DEI

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was a part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that 'Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America'
Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that there is a relentless assault against Black people on behalf of white people
Charlie Kirk admitted that TPUSA had accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry
Charlie Kirk spoke out against targeting fossil fuels and opposed student campaigns that pressured universities to divest from fossil fuels
Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens claimed in a Turning Point USA video that there is no factual data to back up global warming and that scientists do not know the cause
Charlie Kirk called the statement that climate change is an existential threat complete gibberish nonsense and stated that he did not believe human activity is the driver of climate change
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism and likening it to pseudo-paganism

Charlie Kirk said that 'the southern border matters a lot more than the Ukrainian border' and 'I want every Republican leader ... to call what's happening on the southern border an invasion because two million people waltzed into our country last year.'

Charlie Kirk blamed the Chinese government and drug cartels for the opioid crisis in the United States, telling the audience that 'almost nobody in this audience has a friend that you've lost to the Russian government but you do have a friend or a family member that has died because of the cartels and the Chinese Communist Party with a fentanyl coming into our communities'.

Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the attack, and that Benjamin Netanyahu allowed it to go ahead as part of a plan to remain in power.
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation.
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a “stand down” order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating 'very few Americans want war with you' and that 'the people obsessed with fighting you forever' were a minority 'on their way out of power'.
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster' who 'sent his own people to a senseless massacre', claiming that Zelenskyy had no interest in ending the war.

Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel, saying the bill would 'only create more antisemitism, and play into growing narratives that Israel is running the U.S. government'.
Charlie Kirk said of Palestine, 'I don't think the place exists.'
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine, saying: 'We were against it with Biden. Why would we be for it now? Unless it gets us to a peace settlement'.
Charlie Kirk said 'White individuals are actually more likely to be attacked, especially even per capita, by Black individuals in this country'
Charlie Kirk hosted White House border czar Tom Homan on his show, where Homan suggested the presence of immigration enforcement at polling places, asking what Democrats are 'afraid of'.

Charlie Kirk was not mentioned in the court case State of Texas, the Texas Facilities Commission, the Texas Health and Human Services Commission, Mike Novak, in His Official Capacity as Executive Director of the TFC, and Rolland Niles, in His Official Capacity as Deputy Executive Commissioner for the System Support Services Division of the Texas Health and Human Services Commission v. Broadmoor Austin Associates, a Texas Joint Venture.
Charlie Kirk was not mentioned in the court case State v. B. Hillious.
Charlie Kirk was not mentioned in the court case Murthy v. Missouri.
